name: Rendang (Dry Beef Curry)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine 1/3 cup hot water and either 2 tablespoons packaged tamarind pulp or 4-5 inch whole tamarind pod (remove shell and coarse strings). Let stand for 30 minutes. Knead the pulp from seeds: discard seeds. Use 1/2 cup hot water and 1/4 cup pulp to prepare the tamarind liquid. Set aside.
2. Heat oil in a wok or 5-quart kettle over medium-high heat. Add meat, a few pieces at a time, and cook until browned on all sides. Remove onion when soft. Add coriander, cinnamon stick, cumin, Chili paste, pepper, cloves, and anchovy paste (if used). Cook, stirring, until seasonings are well combined.
3. Return beef to the kettle; stir to coat with spice mixture. Stir in tamarind liquid and coconut milk. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low and simmer, stirring occasionally, until meat is very tender when pierced and sauce is very thick and almost dry (1 1/2 to 2 hours).
4. Remove from heat and discard cinnamon stick. Spoon off and discard fat from the sauce. Serve with rice.
